description: Tokens the Builder V2 prompt assembler recognises inside an addon's `config.prompt`. Source of truth for: server promptAssembler.js, client MentionTextarea picker, Alfred (patchGenerator/bodyValidator/alfredRunner system prompts).

trigger_prefixes.@: memory (whole section, single domain, or single field)
trigger_prefixes.*: enum bible — aggregate-by-section across every value of an enum, OR live-value lookup for an enum-typed field
trigger_prefixes.%: summary (whole section or single summarizer slot — written by offline-lane Summarizer addons)
trigger_prefixes.+: snippet (agent-level reusable prompt content, optionally gated by an AddonFilter)
trigger_prefixes.&: tags (fields grouped by tag — schema block, current values, or bare names)
trigger_prefixes.~: kb retrieval (the named slot a KB Retriever addon wrote this turn)
trigger_prefixes.{{: unified picker — every placeholder from every category in one list. Use when you don't remember which sigil opens what.
trigger_prefixes./: alias for `{{` — same unified picker, single-keystroke trigger. Common pattern in command-bar UIs.

sections.render: Full ## Memory dump as markdown. Every domain whose fields have any non-null values, each rendered as a ### sub-block of JSON. Empty if no memory has been written this conversation.

Every persona applicable to THIS addon (its appliesTo includes the addon's pluginId or '*'), concatenated in the Personas-page order. One → ## Persona heading; several → ## Persona: NAME blocks. Empty if none apply.
The content of one specific persona by name, inline (no heading). Regardless of its appliesTo. Left in place if NAME doesn't exist.
Full ## Thinking dump as markdown. Every thinking domain whose values exist, each as a ### sub-block.
Full ## Summary dump as markdown. Every declared summarizer with a current text, rendered as a ### NAME sub-block. Empty if no summarizer has fired yet.

domains.render: One memory domain as a ### DOMAIN block with its JSON. Used when you want a specific bucket without the whole ## Memory wrapper.
One thinking domain as a ### DOMAIN block with its JSON.

values.render: The current memory value for a single field, inline. Bare value with no label or formatting. Empty string when the field has no value yet.
The configured value of an agent parameter. Static — never changes per conversation. Empty string when the parameter is undefined.
values.example: Welcome to {{param:bankName}}. Call {{param:supportPhone}} if you need help.

values.render: Aggregate dump of every value's umbrella prompt for the named enum. Wrapped in a `## NAME` header, with each value rendered as a `### value` sub-block. Values with empty umbrella text are omitted. Use this when an extractor / reasoner prompt needs the per-value briefing for every possible value of an enum (e.g. `Possible motives: {{enum:motive}}`).

Aggregate dump of one declared SECTION across every value of the named enum. Wrapped in a `## NAME — SECTION` header, with each value rendered as a `### value` sub-block. Values with no body for that section are omitted. Section bodies can be many paragraphs — the renderer preserves whitespace.

INLINE comma-separated list of the named enum's value names. Use in prose like `Allowed values: {{enum:motive:values}}`. The reserved keyword `values` is forbidden as a section name on the editor side so the two never collide. Empty when the enum has no declared values; literal token when the enum doesn't exist (typo surfaces loudly).

Looks up FIELD (must be an enum-typed field), resolves its current memory value to a value record on its enum, and renders that value's umbrella prompt bare (no wrapping headers — author adds their own preamble). Resolves to empty when the field has no value or the value isn't declared on the enum.
Same lookup as `{{dc:FIELD}}` but renders the matched value's SECTION body bare. Empty when the field is unset, the value isn't on the enum, or that value has no body for the named section.

Joins every declared section under the matched value as `### section_name` blocks separated by blank lines. Convenience form for 'give me everything authored under the current value of this field'. Sections with empty bodies are omitted.
The current text of one summarizer slot, inline (bare — no `### NAME` heading; wrap in your own copy if you want one). Empty string when the named summarizer hasn't fired yet or no longer exists.

Agent-level reusable prompt content, inline (bare text). Resolves to the snippet's `content` when its optional filter (if any) matches; otherwise empty string. Empty when the snippet doesn't exist. The snippet substitution runs FIRST in the assembler — so a snippet's content can itself reference `{{field:X}}` / `{{memory}}` / `{{dynamic:Z}}` / etc. and have those resolve on the regular passes. Nested `{{snippet:OTHER}}` references inside snippet content show up as literal text (no recursive expansion in v1).

Schema-style labeled block listing every field tagged NAME — one bullet per field with `name — howToExtract`. Wrapped in `### tag — NAME`. Use when a prompt needs to say 'pay attention to these fields' across fields from different domains. Empty when no field carries the tag.

INLINE comma-separated `name: value` pairs of the current memory values for every field tagged NAME. Skips fields with null/undefined values. The field name is always included so values can't be misread.
INLINE comma-separated bare field names tagged NAME — the variables themselves, no values. Useful when referencing the set of variables in prose.

The text a KB Retriever addon wrote to its named slot (`config.domain`) earlier this turn — retrieved KB chunks (or the retriever's configured empty-sentinel). Never blank: a missing/empty slot renders a generic 'no relevant information' sentinel so prompts like 'answer only from {{kb:docs}}' stay coherent. Ephemeral — recomputed every turn, never persisted.

extractor_only.render: Schema block for the fields this extractor collects (one bullet per field with type, source, allowed enum values, and how-to-extract). Only renders inside extractor plugins; empty everywhere else.
JSON map of the extractor's fields that currently hold non-null values. Only renders inside extractor plugins.

Literal NAME of the (first) field this extractor populates. Designed for single-field extractors (Field Reasoner): use it to tell the LLM what it's being asked to infer (`You are inferring the value of {{this_field}}`) and as the JSON key (`{ "{{this_field}}": <value> }`). Renders empty for non-extractor plugins or when the extractor has no fields.
extractor_only.example: You are inferring the value of `{{this_field}}`.

extractor_only.render: Comma-separated list of values of the (first) extracted field's enum type. Resolved from `FieldDef.enumType` → that EnumTypeDef's `values[].value`. Single source of truth lives on the enum bible. Renders empty when the field is non-enum, has no `enumType` set, or the referenced enum has no values declared.

idioms.name: Inline value in prose
idioms.use_when: You want a specific value woven into natural language.
idioms.snippet: The customer's name is {{field:customer_name}}. They live in {{field:customer_city}}.

idioms.name: Scoped memory dump
idioms.use_when: You want only one slice of memory, not everything.

idioms.name: Whole-section context block
idioms.use_when: You want the full picture and don't care to enumerate.

idioms.name: Static config substitution
idioms.use_when: Bank name, support phone, regulatory string — anything fixed at agent config time.
idioms.snippet: You are a customer support agent for {{param:bankName}}.

idioms.name: Live-value guidance from the enum bible
idioms.use_when: Behaviour should swap based on a deterministically extracted enum field — e.g. switching on intent, mood, plan tier. The field's current value is looked up against its declared enum and the matched value's umbrella is dropped in.
idioms.snippet:
Use this guidance for the current case:

{{dc:intent}}

idioms.name: Per-value identification briefing for an extractor
idioms.use_when: An extractor / reasoner needs every value's how-to-identify guidance up-front so the LLM can pick the right one. The enum aggregate token emits one block per declared value with a stable header structure.
idioms.snippet:
Possible motives and how to spot each:

{{enum:motive:how_to_identify}}

idioms.name: Section-targeted live-value guidance
idioms.use_when: A single matched value (e.g. `mood == sad`) needs multiple discrete chunks of guidance for different consumers — opening line, what to avoid, how to address. Sections on the enum value let each prompt cherry-pick the slice it needs.

idioms.name: All sections under the current matched value
idioms.use_when: You want every authored section under the matching enum value dropped in as headed blocks — the 'give me everything' form.
